evidence_excerpt: At last evaluation (age 20), the patient had moderate intellectual disability with persistent exercise-induced muscular pain and fatigue, elevated CK, and generalized epilepsy controlled on monotherapy; cardiac and endocrine evaluations were normal.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing muscular pains during physical exercises with severe fatigue; epilepsy present but well controlled with monotherapy.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Since age 10, muscular pains during physical exercises, initially in the feet then extending to the legs and hands, associated with severe muscular fatigue.
Generalized epilepsy since age 16 years, with a good response to monotherapy.

notes_on_uncertainty: The report describes two patients; this extraction focuses on Patient 2 due to adult follow-up (age 20). Exact diagnosis timing is not provided, so events are assigned as unknown relative to index time. Long-term outcome reflects stable chronic symptoms without documented progression or recovery beyond seizure control.
